title: criteria.json — oracle behavioral criteria
description: OUR machine-checkable, product-contract behaviors used to score the installed software (§6, dimension 1). Hidden from both the Seed Creator and the Seed Installer. The file records its OWN expected count (`count`) so the Evaluator discovers N rather than hardcoding it; validation asserts count == criteria.length (validate.mjs --assert-count count:criteria). The per-criterion `check` object is intentionally open: its concrete shapes are defined by the Evaluator (Chunk 2). Criteria content for oh-my-logo is authored in Chunk 3; this schema is the Chunk-1 deliverable.

note.description: OPTIONAL free-text provenance/authoring note for the file (e.g. how the criteria were derived).

count.description: Self-declared N; MUST equal criteria.length (enforced by validate.mjs --assert-count).

description.description: What product-visible behavior this verifies — never implementation trivia.

tier.description: gate = a hard gate (build/launch/core-action) whose failure forces the graded score to 0; graded = a normal scored criterion.

check.description: How the Evaluator verifies this criterion. The `type` selects a checker; remaining fields are checker-specific (defined in Chunk 2). Left open here (additionalProperties allowed).
